name: Orbit Notes
oneLineDescription: A lightweight team workspace for capturing meeting notes, decisions, and action items
whatItIs: Orbit Notes is a lightweight team workspace for capturing meeting notes, decisions, and action items. It helps small teams turn messy conversations into clear next steps. The primary use case is a team lead creates a note during or after a meeting, captures key decisions, assigns action items to teammates, and everyone can later search, filter, and track progress.
coreUserFlows: Team lead creates a note during or after a meeting and captures key decisions
Team lead assigns action items to teammates from meeting notes
Team members search, filter, and track progress on action items
Users can search across all notes using keywords to find relevant content

dataModel.purpose: Stores user accounts with authentication credentials
Stores team information and display names
Stores project containers for organizing notes
Stores meeting notes with rich text content
Stores tasks extracted from notes with assignment and status tracking

crossCuttingPrinciples: All users belong to exactly one team and all data access is scoped to the user's team
Three-tier role system with Viewer (read-only), Contributor (create/edit), and Team Lead (full management) permissions
Teams must always have at least one Team Lead to prevent orphaned teams
Rich text editing is limited to basic formatting: headings, bold, italic, strikethrough, and lists only
All notes must belong to a project and all action items must belong to a note to prevent orphaned content
specSummary: Orbit Notes is a team-focused note-taking and task management application designed to help small teams capture meeting outcomes and track follow-up actions. The application centers around a single-team workspace model where users have role-based permissions (Viewer, Contributor, Team Lead) that control their ability to create, edit, and manage content. The core workflow involves creating notes within projects, extracting action items from those notes, and assigning tasks to team members for completion tracking. The technical foundation uses Next.js 14 with Supabase for backend services including PostgreSQL database, authentication, and real-time subscriptions. Key features include rich text note editing with basic formatting, project-based organization, action item assignment and filtering, keyword search across note content, activity timeline tracking, and comprehensive team member management. The application explicitly excludes complex features like multi-team membership, file attachments, real-time collaborative editing, and notification systems in favor of maintaining simplicity and focus on the core note-to-action workflow that small teams need most.
